metadata.title: Google Nano Banana Edit — Image Editing AI | AI Image Editor Free
metadata.description: Nano Banana Edit on Kie.ai: reference-driven image editing. Docs, workflow tips, and how to run edits in our image generator.

page.title: Nano Banana Edit
page.description: Nano Banana Edit expects reference images and prompt instructions to apply targeted edits while preserving intent from the source photo.

hero.title: Nano Banana Edit — Image-to-Image
hero.description: Use Nano Banana Edit when you need to change outfits, lighting, or objects while keeping the original composition. Reference images are required for this route.
hero.tip: Docs: Kie.ai Nano Banana Edit →

images.title: Before / after style examples
images.description: Placeholder images — tag showcases with nano-banana-edit for real examples.

images.prompt: Change jacket color to navy, keep pose

capabilities.title: Why Nano Banana Edit
capabilities.description: Purpose-built for editing workflows instead of starting from a blank canvas.

items.title: Reference-first
items.description: Edits are anchored to your uploaded images for more controllable results.

items.title: Instruction following
items.description: Describe precise changes without re-specifying the entire scene.

items.title: Pairs with T2I
items.description: Use alongside Nano Banana text-to-image when you need both creation and retouching.

items.question: Why does the UI ask for reference images?
items.answer: This model route is designed for image-to-image editing; upload at least one reference image before generating.

cta.title: Try image editing
cta.description: Switch to image-to-image in the generator and select Nano Banana Edit.
